items.headline: Wall Street Week | Paulson on Iran War, Energy Market Disruptions, Copper Supply Strain, Tax Debate
items.summary: This week, Former Treasury Secretary Hank Paulson warns the biggest economic risk from the Iran war may come from global shocks spilling into US markets.  Plus, How rising demand for copper is outpacing supply, exposing gaps in US production and increasing reliance on imports. Later, are the wealthiest Americans paying their fair share, or does the tax system allow too much income to go untaxed? (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Geothermal Power Firm Fervo Energy Files for IPO
items.summary: Fervo Energy Co. filed for an initial public offering, disclosing wider losses as it plans to start generating power from its first geothermal project in Utah later this year.

items.headline: Blue Owl Co-CEOs’ Personal Loans No Longer Backed by Firm Shares
items.summary: Blue Owl Capital Inc. Co-Chief Executive Officers Doug Ostrover and Marc Lipschultz have revised the terms of personal loans to remove the company’s shares as collateral, after turmoil in the private credit market hammered the stock’s value in recent months.

items.headline: REAL Cookies Founders on The Future of Healthy Snaking
items.summary: Lauren Berger and Marla Felton, REAL Cookies Co-Founders joined Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss their merger with Creations Foods and the outlook for healthy snacking. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Rosner on Credit Markets Amid Geopolitical Tension
items.summary: Lindsay Rosner, Head of Multi Sector Fixed Income at Goldman Sachs Asset Management, discuss the current state of credit markets amid geopolitical tensions and private credit challenges. Rosner highlights that while credit spreads remain tight, the all-in yield remains attractive to investors. She speaks with Katie Greifeld on “Real Yield.” (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Oil Tankers Hauling US Crude Via Panama Approach 4-Year High
items.summary: US oil cargoes transiting the Panama Canal are close to a four-year high as Asian refiners rush to import American crude in lieu of Mideast supplies strangled by the weeks-long disruption to Strait of Hormuz shipping.

items.headline: KKR-Backed Emergency Services Firm GMR Files for US IPO
items.summary: GMR Solutions Inc., the KKR & Co.-backed air and ground emergency medical services company, filed for a US initial public offering as it joins a growing list of firms seeking to tap investors.

items.headline: Oil Tankers Make Dash Toward Hormuz as Iran Says Strait Is Open
items.summary: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is open — and oil tankers are racing through it. Norah Mulinda explains how markets reacted to the news and asks: Can this lead to a lasting peace agreement? (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Biotech Odyssey, Medical Device Firm Mobia File for US IPOs
items.summary: Healthcare companies are lining up to go public with a drug developer and medical device company joining a pool of peers seeking to advance public listing plans amid robust investor demand for biotech stocks.

items.headline: Live Nation Inks $742 Million of Private Debt Tied to Venues
items.summary: Live Nation Entertainment Inc. has borrowed about €630 million ($742 million) in the private debt market to help finance its global investment in concert venues, people with knowledge of the matter said.

items.headline: Blackstone-Backed Ad Tech Firm Liftoff Mobile Refiles for US IPO
items.summary: Liftoff Mobile Inc. filed again for an initial public offering just two months after withdrawing a previous registration.

items.headline: IMF, World Bank Navigate Economic Fallout From Iran War
items.summary: With the world facing a potential new economic crisis, the mood at this week’s IMF-World Bank meetings is decidedly dour. Bloomberg Economics’ Stephanie Flanders and Shawn Donnan join David Gura to discuss on the Big Take podcast. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: AI Chipmaker Cerebras Systems Files Publicly for US IPO
items.summary: Cerebras Systems Inc., an artificial intelligence chipmaker and data center operator, filed publicly for an initial public offering months after withdrawing a previous attempt to list.

items.headline: Oil and Gas Plunge on Hormuz Opening, Hope for End of War
items.summary: Oil and European natural gas prices tumbled after the promised re-opening of the Strait of Hormuz bolstered sweeping optimism that the US-Iran conflict will reach an end and ease disruptions to global energy markets.

items.headline: NYC’s Second-Home Tax Unites Mamdani, Hochul
items.summary: Bloomberg's Nacha Cattan joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield."  Governor Kathy Hochul and New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani have finally found a tax that they agree on. Getting everyone else on board stands to be more complicated. Mamdani has been an eager proponent of taxing wealthier New Yorkers to close a projected $5.4 billion two-year gap in the city’s budget and to fund a slew of social programs.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Belgium Gets Cut by Moody’s in Reproof at Failure to Cut Debt
items.summary: Belgium was lowered one step by Moody’s, underscoring how the struggle to cut one of the biggest budget deficits in Europe is besmirching the country’s status as a borrower.

items.headline: Gold Jumps to One-Month High as Iran Says Hormuz Completely Open
items.summary: Gold jumped to the highest in almost a month after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz “completely open” to commercial traffic, a major step toward ending the conflict that spurred energy prices to rally and threatened economic growth.

items.headline: Pakistan’s Army Chief Takes Spotlight in US-Iran Peace Talks
items.summary:
Pakistan has emerged as the unlikely facilitator of US-Iran peace negotiations, and with the prospect of additional talks looming, the man at the center of those efforts is the country’s army chief.
Field Marshal Asim Munir, who has positioned himself as the most powerful man in Pakistan, is the favored interlocutor between Washington and Tehran as the warring nations weigh whether to extend their two-week ceasefire due to expire next week.  Mishal Husain, Host of the 'The Mishal Husain Show' joins to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Trump Says Iran Will Suspend Nuclear Program as Hormuz Reopens
items.summary:
President Donald Trump said Iran has agreed to suspend its nuclear program indefinitely, fueling optimism that a deal to end their war is in reach after Tehran announced the Strait of Hormuz was open again.
In a Friday phone interview, Trump asserted that any moratorium on Iran’s nuclear activities would not expire, saying it would be “unlimited.” Talks over a lasting agreement will “probably” be held this weekend, the president said. Spencer Faragasso, Institute for Science and International Security Senior Fellow joined Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Private Credit Dispersion Creates Opportunity: Cocke
items.summary: John Cocke, deputy CIO for credit at Corbin Capital Partners, joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Private credit funds run by some of the biggest managers are contending with a surge in redemption requests amid concerns about loan quality and exposure to borrowers vulnerable to AI disruption. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Wall Street Turns Gloomy on the Dollar as Haven Demand Fades
items.summary: Deutsche Bank AG and Wells Fargo & Co. say the dollar’s war-driven haven rally is likely over, as the US-Iran ceasefire pushes investors back into riskier assets.

items.headline: S&P 500 Closes at Record High | Closing Bell
items.summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Wall Street Extends April Surge on Peace Prospects: Markets Wrap
items.summary: Speculation the Iran war is poised to end sent risky assets surging anew, extending a month-long rally that’s lifted the S&P 500 Index to a succession of records, added around $12,000 to the price of Bitcoin and pushed up credit and gold.

items.headline: Wall Street Rushes to Price Out War Damage as Stocks Hit Records
items.summary: Traders spent this week betting that the US conflict with Iran is all but over — driving stocks to records, dumping the dollar and pushing oil to around $90 a barrel.

items.headline: Drone Maker Aevex Shares Climb 35% After $320 Million US IPO
items.summary: Aevex Corp. shares rose 35% after the maker of military drones raised $320 million in a US initial public offering.

items.headline: Warsh Fed Chair Hearing Set for Next Week
items.summary: Bloomberg's Enda Curran joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." The Senate Banking Committee has scheduled a confirmation hearing at 10 a.m. on April 21 for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Obesity Drugmaker Kailera Soars 63% After $625 Million IPO
items.summary: Kailera Therapeutics Inc. shares surged 63% after the clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on obesity raised $625 million in an upsized US initial public offering.

items.headline: Medical Device Maker Alamar Bio Jumps 29% After Upsized IPO
items.summary: Alamar Biosciences Inc. shares climbed 29% on Friday after the medical device maker raised $191 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced at the top of a marketed range.

items.headline: 'Permanent Scarring' From Iran War: Goldman's Rosner
items.summary: Lindsay Rosner, head of multi-sector team at Goldman Sachs Asset Management, joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Credit investors who gambled on higher-yielding corporate bonds in the midst of the Iran war are looking increasingly vindicated as markets rebound as the Strait of Hormuz reopens. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Uruguay Central Bank Chief Sees Better Growth in First Quarter
items.summary: Uruguay’s economy should bounce back from weak growth in the second half of last year to expand by just under 1% in this year’s first quarter, according to the country’s top central banker.

events.event_name: U.S. dollar seen strengthening as U.S.-Israel strikes intensify
OPEC+ agrees modest oil output boost even as US war on Iran disrupts shipments
